ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.1 This sub-section defines the core environmental and physical requirements for all EIRENE mobile equipment. The requirements provided in this section are augmented by those provided in later sections for each individual radio type, with each radio type being specified by the superset of the core plus specific requirements. (I) 4.3.1 This sub-section defines the core environmental and physical requirements for all EIRENE mobile equipment. The requirements provided in this section are augmented by those provided in later sections for each individual radio type, with each radio type being specified by the superset of the core plus specific requirements. (I)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.2 The categories of requirements defined for each type of mobile equipment are as follows: (I) 4.3.2 The categories of requirements defined for each type of mobile equipment are as follows: (I) climatic conditions (temperature, humidity, solar radiation, altitude, etc); physical conditions (flammability, contamination, physical protection, etc); mechanical conditions (shock and vibration); electrical conditions (power supply variation, battery life, overloading, etc); EMC (both emissions and immunity).
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.3 In normal operation of a mobile radio unit, it is expected that a combination of the above environmental conditions will be experienced. (I) 4.3.3 In normal operation of a mobile radio unit, it is expected that a combination of the above environmental conditions will be experienced. (I)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.4 Any environmental and physical requirements stated may be superseded by national requirements if the national standards provide a higher level of environmental and physical protection. However, stricter national standards shall not prevent the use of other EIRENE mobiles in that country. (M) 4.3.4 Any environmental and physical requirements stated may be superseded by national requirements if the national standards provide a higher level of environmental and physical protection. However, stricter national standards shall not prevent the use of other EIRENE mobiles in that country. (M)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.5 All design, testing and installation of EIRENE mobile radio equipment shall comply with the quality procedures defined in ISO 9001 . (M) Climatic conditions 4.3.5 All design, testing and installation of EIRENE mobile radio equipment shall comply with the quality procedures defined in ISO 9001 . (M) Climatic conditions
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.6 Any pressure pulses caused by vehicles passing, entering or leaving tunnels shall not affect the normal operation of EIRENE mobile equipment. ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.7 The design of EIRENE mobiles shall comply with the relevant European Norms for protection against physical hazards, eg: (M) 4.3.7 The design of EIRENE mobiles shall comply with the relevant European Norms for protection against physical hazards, eg: (M) pollution; chemical contaminants (eg diesel); flammable substances; biologically active substances; dust, sand and heavy particles; mould growth. Mechanical conditions
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.8 All EIRENE mobile equipment shall be capable of being operated on board rolling stock. (M) Electrical 4.3.8 All EIRENE mobile equipment shall be capable of being operated on board rolling stock. (M) Electrical
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.9 The batteries to be used in the mobile equipment shall have sufficient capacity to support typical railway use. ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.10 EIRENE mobiles will generate electromagnetic emissions. It is the responsibility of national railways operating EIRENE networks to ensure that EIRENE equipment does not interfere with the normal operation of any on-train or ground-based systems. (I) 4.3.10 EIRENE mobiles will generate electromagnetic emissions. It is the responsibility of national railways operating EIRENE networks to ensure that EIRENE equipment does not interfere with the normal operation of any on-train or ground-based systems. (I)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 4.3.11 In particular, EIRENE equipment could interfere with: (I) 4.3.11 In particular, EIRENE equipment could interfere with: (I) signalling relays and contacts; power transformers; track circuits; axle counters; train describers; other radio equipment; radar speed measurement equipment; switched mode power supplies; telecommunications circuits; electronic locking systems.

ground_truth_requirements.text: 5.2.1.1 In this subsection, the functions to be provided by the Cab radio are described. (I) 5.2.1.1 In this subsection, the functions to be provided by the Cab radio are described. (I)

ground_truth_requirements.text: 5.2.1.2 The following functions will be provided: Driver call-related functions: 5.2.1.2 The following functions will be provided: Driver call-related functions: call controller: (M) primary controller; secondary controller; power supply controller; call other drivers in the area; (M) send Railway emergency call; (M) confirm receipt of Railway emergency call; (M) communicate with other drivers on same train; (M) call train staff; (M) call other authorised users; (M) receive incoming voice calls; (M) terminate calls; (M) receive text messages; (M) enter/leave shunting mode; (M) enter/leave direct mode; (O) monitor calls to other on-train users/devices; (M) forward calls/cancel call forwarding to/from driver handheld; (O) Other driver-related functions: powering up radio; (M) switch radio MMI on and off; (M) select language; (M) adjust loudspeaker volume; (M) select mobile radio network; (M) register and deregister train number; (M) register and deregister on-train users; (O) register and deregister stock number; (M) store/retrieve numbers and their details; (M) invoke supplementary services; (M) invoke tests; (M) Other Cab radio functions: automatic connection of incoming calls to appropriate on-train users or devices (conductor, public address system, data systems, etc); (M) automatic establishment of outgoing calls initiated by on-train users or devices; (M) automatic handling of calls of varying priorities; (M) send to the controller(s) a signal on activation of driver safety device; (M) transmit Railway emergency call event indication to train-borne recorder; (M) run-time diagnostics. (O)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

In the latter case, in order to avoid disturbing a shunting movement, the shunting leader should be informed by a visual and audible indication. The shunting leader can then choose the moment when he takes or rejects the call. This facility is required principally for the safe conduct of pushing manoeuvres to assure continuing availability of the radio channel while a shunting worker is guiding a train driver. (I) 14.4.1 The link assurance signal is a means to provide end-to-end confirmation between users that a voice communication link remains intact. This facility is required principally for the safe conduct of pushing manoeuvres to assure continuing availability of the radio channel while a shunting worker is guiding a train driver. (I)

ground_truth_requirements.text: 14.4.2 The link assurance signal shall consist of an intermittent audio tone (between 800 and 850Hz). The tone shall be of one second duration, followed by an interval of two seconds. (M) 14.4.2 The link assurance signal shall consist of an intermittent audio tone (between 800 and 850Hz). The tone shall be of one second duration, followed by an interval of two seconds. (M)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 14.4.3 In order to fulfil the requirements of some railways, it should be possible to provide an alternative means of link assurance indication. ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 14.4.4 This means will consist of an additional safety device (external to the radio system) interposed between the Cab radio and the loudspeaker with the purpose of muting the continuous link assurance signal for the drivers convenience. In this case, the safety device will alert the driver only if the link assurance signal has failed or if it is cancelled. (I) 14.4.4 This means will consist of an additional safety device (external to the radio system) interposed between the Cab radio and the loudspeaker with the purpose of muting the continuous link assurance signal for the drivers convenience. ground_truth_requirements.text: 14.4.4i Operational radios used for shunting shall be able to be switched between the following two operating modes by a maintenance function: (M) 14.4.4i Operational radios used for shunting shall be able to be switched between the following two operating modes by a maintenance function: (M) Mode A: the link assurance signal shall be interrupted, but not cancelled, when the initiator of the link assurance signal presses the PTT to speak. Mode B: the link assurance signal shall not be interrupted when the initiator of the link assurance signal presses the PTT to speak.

ground_truth_requirements.text: 14.4.5 The link assurance signal shall be deactivated upon receipt of a shunting emergency call. (M) 14.4.5 The link assurance signal shall be deactivated upon receipt of a shunting emergency call. (M)

ground_truth_requirements.text: 14.4.6 All members of the shunting group, other than the initiator of the link assurance signal, shall be unable to speak during the transmission of the link assurance signal. However, they shall be able to initiate a shunting emergency call at any time. (M)

ground_truth_requirements.text: 14.5.1 Operating flexibility: 14.5.1 Operating flexibility: it shall be possible to modify a shunting groups composition during a working period. The process for acceptance (or rejection) of new members, and for admission and removal of temporary members, shall be simple. (M)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 14.5.2 Management flexibility: 14.5.2 Management flexibility: in a given area, a radio set shall be capable of operation in any of the shunting groups in use in that area, subject to any user access restrictions. (M)

ground_truth_requirements.text: 15.1.1 The operational requirement for direct mode is to: (I) 15.1.1 The operational requirement for direct mode is to: (I) provide short range fall-back communications between train drivers and trackside personnel in the event of failure of mobile telephony services normally available; provide short range communications for railway personnel operating in remote areas where no mobile telephony facilities are available.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 15.2.1 Implementation of direct mode is optional. Where the facility is provided, the following requirements are mandatory. (O) 15.2.1 Implementation of direct mode is optional. Where the facility is provided, the following requirements are mandatory. (O)
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 15.2.2 Principal requirements are: (M see above) 15.2.2 Principal requirements are: (M see above) a range of up to at least 2000m in open terrain between a direct mode transmitter and receiver; a voice only capability that supports the use of the link assurance signal; open channel mode of operation such that all users employing direct mode receive transmissions when in range of the transmitting user; it is sufficient that the user may talk or listen only, but not both. The ability to talk shall be achieved through use of the Push-To-Talk function; a minimum of one channel is to be available for use of direct mode facilities; if more than one channel is provided, there must be a function allowing the user to manually select the channel; where equipment provides both normal and direct mode capability, the user shall be able to switch between the two modes in a straightforward manner (but not by accident); the user may only select direct mode when the normal mobile telephony services are not available; controls shall be simple to use, eg: direct mode on/off switch; Push-To-Talk function; channel selection; volume control; battery warning for handportables; battery life for handportables operating in direct mode shall be a minimum of 8 hours based on the following cycle: 20% transmit; 20% receive; 60% stand-by; when direct mode is used as a fall-back, an audible and visual indication shall be provided of normal mobile telephony services returning.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide movement authorities to the train driver.
The system shall display speed guidance to the train driver.
The system shall issue safety alerts to the train driver when necessary.
The system shall calculate braking curves based on track conditions and train parameters.
The system shall enforce speed limits based on movement authorities.
The system shall enforce distance limits based on trackside equipment signals.
The system shall automatically apply emergency brakes when a train exceeds permitted speed parameters.
The system shall automatically apply service brakes when a train passes a stop signal without authority.
The system shall manage transitions between operational levels based on track conditions and national values.
The system shall adapt to local track conditions and national values received from trackside equipment.
The system shall maintain a consistent minimum level of protection throughout all operational levels.
The system shall record operational data including train position, speed, and driver actions for safety auditing purposes.
The Driver Machine Interface (DMI) shall display movement authorities in a clear and understandable manner.
The DMI shall provide audible alerts for critical safety information.
The system shall ensure the integrity of recorded operational data to support accurate incident analysis.
The system shall maintain high availability to ensure continuous operation of the train.
The system shall have a recovery time of less than 5 minutes in case of failure to minimize disruption.
The system shall be secure against unauthorized access and tampering.
The system shall maintain the confidentiality of recorded operational data.
The system shall be designed for easy maintenance and troubleshooting.
The system shall provide detailed error logs to aid in diagnosis of issues.
The system shall be portable across different train types and national rail networks.
The system shall support interoperability with other European Train Control Systems.
The system shall comply with relevant safety standards and regulations, including EN 50128 and EN 50653.
The system shall provide real-time communication between the onboard equipment and trackside infrastructure.
The system shall support continuous communication with Radio Block Centres (RBCs) for safe train movement.
The system shall adapt to changes in trackside equipment signals and national values received from RBCs.
The system shall provide a safe method for the driver to acknowledge movement authorities.
The system shall ensure that the train does not exceed the maximum permitted speed at any point on its route.
The system shall enforce a safe braking distance based on track conditions and train parameters.
The system shall provide early warning to the driver of approaching speed restrictions or stops.
The system shall automatically stop the train if it approaches a stop signal without authority.
The system shall provide a safe method for the driver to request additional movement authorities.
The system shall ensure that the train does not enter a section of track where movement is not authorized.
The system shall provide a safe method for the driver to request a change in operational level.
The system shall ensure that the train operates at an appropriate operational level based on track conditions and national values.
The system shall provide a safe method for the driver to override automatic braking in case of emergency.
The system shall record all safety-critical events for incident analysis purposes.
The system shall provide a safe method for the driver to acknowledge and respond to safety alerts.
The system shall ensure that recorded operational data is time-stamped accurately.
The system shall provide a user-friendly interface for maintenance personnel to access and analyze recorded operational data.
The system shall be designed to minimize false positives in safety alerts to avoid unnecessary disruption.
The system shall provide a safe method for the driver to override automatic braking in case of temporary track obstruction.
The system shall ensure that the train does not enter a section of track where maintenance work is being carried out.
The system shall provide early warning to the driver of approaching level crossings and other hazards.
The system shall ensure that recorded operational data is stored securely to prevent tampering or loss.
The system shall provide a safe method for the driver to request additional movement authorities in case of emergency.
The system shall ensure that the train operates safely during power failures or other temporary disruptions.
The system shall provide a safe method for the driver to request a change in operational level due to temporary track conditions.
The system shall ensure that recorded operational data is archived and made available for long-term analysis.

ground_truth_requirements.text: ETCS shall provide the driver with information to allow him to drive the train safely. ETCS shall provide the driver with information to allow him to drive the train safely.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: ETCS shall be able to supervise train and shunting movements. ETCS shall be able to supervise train and shunting movements.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the supervision is performed by a RBC it shall be possible to prevent movements of a traction unit in its area if not authorised by the RBC. If the supervision is performed by a RBC it shall be possible to prevent movements of a traction unit in its area if not authorised by the RBC.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: ETCS is required to be functional to a maximum train speed of 500 km/h. ETCS is required to be functional to a maximum train speed of 500 km/h.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The following definitions shall apply for the ETCS application levels: The following definitions shall apply for the ETCS application levels: Level 0: ETCS active for limited train control function; trackside not fitted with any train control system or fitted with a train control system for which no STM is available onboard. Level 1: Basic track to train information via intermittent transmission media, e.g. balises. This information can be supported by infill, transmitted via balise, loop or radio. Level 2: Basic track to train and train to track information via continuous transmission media, i.e. radio. The train detection is provided by trackside. Level 3: Same as level 2 except that train integrity is provided by onboard and therefore trackside. train detection is optional. Level STM (Specific Transmission Module): Track to train information provided by national system. Onboard functions provided by national system (STM) in co-operation with onboard ETCS.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: It shall be possible to implement one or more of the ETCS application levels on a line. It shall be possible to implement one or more of the ETCS application levels on a line.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 3 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 3, 2, 1 and 0, trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 2 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 2, 1 and 0 and trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 and 0. Trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 3 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 3, 2, 1 and 0, trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 2 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 2, 1 and 0 and trains equipped for ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 shall be able to run on lines equipped with ERTMS/ETCS application level 1 and 0.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The current application level shall be indicated on the DMI. The current application level shall be indicated on the DMI.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The driver shall acknowledge the level transitions, if requested from trackside. If the driver does not acknowledge after the transition the brake shall be applied. If the driver acknowledges afterwards, the brake can be released The driver shall acknowledge the level transitions, if requested from trackside. If the driver does not acknowledge after the transition the brake shall be applied. If the driver acknowledges afterwards, the brake can be released
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: ETCS shall be compatible with existing national systems listed in the CCS TSI such that it does not interfere with the national systems and is not interfered with by the national systems. ETCS shall be compatible with existing national systems listed in the CCS TSI such that it does not interfere with the national systems and is not interfered with by the national systems.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The ETCS trainborne equipment shall be capable of supervising the following operational states: The ETCS trainborne equipment shall be capable of supervising the following operational states: Full Supervision operation Partial Supervision operation Staff Responsible operation On Sight operation Unfitted Line operation Train Trip operation Post Trip operation National operation (STM) Tandem operation Multiple operation Shunting operation Stand By operation Reversing operation
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Any transition which occurs while the train is moving shall in principle occur automatically. Any transition which occurs while the train is moving shall in principle occur automatically.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Transitions which occur while the train is stationary, shall be initiated automatically or manually as appropriate. Transitions which occur while the train is stationary, shall be initiated automatically or manually as appropriate.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If, as a result of an automatic transition, except for transitions to and from National Operation (STM), the responsibility for the driver increases, the ETCS shall seek an acknowledgement from the driver, whether the train is stationary or not. If, as a result of an automatic transition, except for transitions to and from National Operation (STM), the responsibility for the driver increases, the ETCS shall seek an acknowledgement from the driver, whether the train is stationary or not.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: For transitions to and from National Operation (STM) the ETCS shall request, an acknowledgement by the driver. For transitions to and from National Operation (STM) the ETCS shall request, an acknowledgement by the driver.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: In case the transition has to be acknowledged and the driver fails to acknowledge as required, the ETCS shall initiate a brake application In case the transition has to be acknowledged and the driver fails to acknowledge as required, the ETCS shall initiate a brake application
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: During the transition period between two operational states (including two different national operations) the supervision provided shall at least ensure the same protection provided by the least restrictive state. During the transition period between two operational states (including two different national operations) the supervision provided shall at least ensure the same protection provided by the least restrictive state.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If an ETCS equipped train passes a level transition to a line fitted with more than one level, the onboard shall switch to the highest level, according to the priority given by trackside, for which it is equipped. If an ETCS equipped train passes a level transition to a line fitted with more than one level, the onboard shall switch to the highest level, according to the priority given by trackside, for which it is equipped.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If an ETCS equipped train passes a level transition to one or more levels for which it is not equipped, ETCS shall initiate a brake application. If an ETCS equipped train passes a level transition to one or more levels for which it is not equipped, ETCS shall initiate a brake application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The current operational status shall be indicated to the driver on the DMI The current operational status shall be indicated to the driver on the DMI
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The ETCS on-board shall be capable of receiving National values from the trackside to adapt to National requirements The ETCS on-board shall be capable of receiving National values from the trackside to adapt to National requirements
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: National values shall be applicable to a defined area National values shall be applicable to a defined area
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Once received onboard the national values shall remain valid even if the onboard equipment is switched off Once received onboard the national values shall remain valid even if the onboard equipment is switched off
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the on-board has no valid national values for the current location, default values shall be used by the onboard equipment. If the on-board has no valid national values for the current location, default values shall be used by the onboard equipment.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Default values shall be harmonised values, permanently stored in all ERTMS/ETCS on board equipment Default values shall be harmonised values, permanently stored in all ERTMS/ETCS on board equipment
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: At Start Up, the on board equipment shall perform an automatic self-test. At Start Up, the on board equipment shall perform an automatic self-test.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The test shall require no action on the part of the driver. The test shall require no action on the part of the driver.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The DMI shall indicate the result of the self-test. The DMI shall indicate the result of the self-test.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Train data shall be entered before the on-board ETCS equipment allows train movement. Train data shall be entered before the on-board ETCS equipment allows train movement.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The driver shall be able to select Train Data Entry on the DMI. The driver shall be able to select Train Data Entry on the DMI.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Entering or overwriting data manually by the driver shall be possible but only when stationary. Entering or overwriting data manually by the driver shall be possible but only when stationary.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Train data may be entered automatically from a railway management system or from train memory. Train data may be entered automatically from a railway management system or from train memory.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The driver shall be able to consult train data when the train is stationary or moving. The driver shall be able to consult train data when the train is stationary or moving.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Current train data shall be stored (except at transition to shunting) in the ETCS equipment until the traction unit is not operative. Current train data shall be stored (except at transition to shunting) in the ETCS equipment until the traction unit is not operative.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Stored train data shall be offered to the driver to be confirmed when Data Entry starts. Stored train data shall be offered to the driver to be confirmed when Data Entry starts.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system for Train Data Entry shall provide for the input of other data required by STMs connected to ETCS. This may require additional items, not required for ETCS, to be entered. The system for Train Data Entry shall provide for the input of other data required by STMs connected to ETCS. This may require additional items, not required for ETCS, to be entered.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The entry of driver identification and the selection of the language shall be possible. The entry of driver identification and the selection of the language shall be possible.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The change of driver identification during a journey or a Train Running Number shall be possible The change of driver identification during a journey or a Train Running Number shall be possible
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Following successful completion of Train Data Entry, the driver shall be able to perform shunting movements or train movements. Following successful completion of Train Data Entry, the driver shall be able to perform shunting movements or train movements.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR
